jokes.text: Frank eating a sandwich during a serious TV interview about being mugged
jokes.setup: Frank is on morning TV to tell a harrowing story about being mugged
jokes.punchline: He's casually eating a sandwich during the serious interview
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Frank's complete lack of self-awareness and social decorum. Visual comedy that establishes his obliviousness.

jokes.text: I'm starving. You don't have nothing to eat in this show.
jokes.setup: TV host asks Frank to eat his sandwich later
jokes.punchline: Frank complains the show doesn't provide food
jokes.explanation: Frank treating a news interview like a casual visit, completely missing the gravity of the situation.

jokes.text: I don't know if they wanted money or they wanted something more sexual.
jokes.setup: Frank describing his mugging experience
jokes.punchline: He casually suggests the muggers might have wanted to sexually assault him
jokes.explanation: Dark humor typical of the show - Frank's casual mention of potential sexual assault on morning TV.

jokes.text: But it's a lucky thing I had my pieces. Your-your pieces? My guns.
jokes.setup: Frank mentions having his 'pieces'
jokes.punchline: The confused host needs clarification that 'pieces' means guns
jokes.explanation: Frank's street slang confusing the professional TV host - good character-based wordplay.

jokes.text: Anyway, I started blasting. Bah! Bah!
jokes.setup: Frank describing using his gun
jokes.punchline: He makes childish sound effects on live TV
jokes.explanation: Frank's complete inappropriate behavior escalates - making gun sound effects during a serious interview about violence.

jokes.text: Now, I don't see so good, so I missed, then they ran away, I ran after them.
jokes.setup: Frank explaining what happened after he started shooting
jokes.punchline: He admits he can't see well but was shooting anyway
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy about an elderly man with poor vision firing a gun in public - absurd and dangerous.

jokes.text: Tried to shoot them in the back, but I don't run so good either.
jokes.setup: Frank chased the muggers
jokes.punchline: He tried to shoot them in the back while running but can't run well either
jokes.explanation: Escalates the absurdity - Frank casually admits to attempted murder while highlighting his physical limitations. Dark and perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Anyway, you guys all think I'm a hero, and I'll accept that responsibility.
jokes.setup: Frank has just described recklessly firing a gun in public
jokes.punchline: He assumes everyone thinks he's a hero for it
jokes.explanation: Frank's delusional self-perception - he thinks his dangerous behavior makes him heroic.

jokes.text: The whole gun thing, it just makes me really hot. Yeah, well, it's a hot issue and I'm getting hot about it, too. This is good, we're all hot at the same time.
jokes.setup: Characters discussing getting heated about gun issues
jokes.punchline: They keep using 'hot' in increasingly suggestive ways
jokes.explanation: Basic innuendo escalation - functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Guys, we're hot about the complete opposite.
jokes.setup: The gang thinks they're all passionate about the same gun issue
jokes.punchline: Dennis realizes they're on opposite sides
jokes.explanation: Good setup payoff - they were so focused on being 'hot' they didn't realize they disagreed completely.

jokes.text: Which is a bummer, 'cause I thought we were on the same page for once. Still very hot though. Yeah. Oh, I'm even hotter if I'm being honest. Still hot.
jokes.setup: They realize they disagree politically
jokes.punchline: They continue to escalate how 'hot' they are despite the disagreement
jokes.explanation: Continues the joke - they're so focused on their shared 'hotness' they ignore the fundamental disagreement.

jokes.text: You know what I mean? Like, you need a guy in a school that even from a mile away, the school shooter sees that guy and says, 'Uh-uh. I ain't messing with that dude.'
jokes.setup: Mac explaining their tactical outfits
jokes.punchline: He thinks their appearance alone will deter shooters from a mile away
jokes.explanation: Mac's delusional confidence about his intimidating appearance - absurd overestimation of their tactical gear's psychological impact.

jokes.text: This guy was the principal at the high school where I was doing the janitor thing. Yeah. Right, so why are you at a middle school now? Well, I was... I was transferred.
jokes.setup: Charlie recognizes the principal
jokes.punchline: The principal was transferred (implying demotion) because of Charlie
jokes.explanation: Awkward revelation that Charlie's past behavior got someone demoted - good cringe comedy.

jokes.text: No, they do not. That is why I brought along... a saber. Aw, Jesus Christ.
jokes.setup: Charlie agrees guns don't belong in schools
jokes.punchline: He pulls out a sword instead
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Charlie logic - avoiding guns by bringing a medieval weapon to school. Great escalation and character moment.

jokes.text: Ah. I guess I do have a hair trigger, huh?
jokes.setup: Mac accidentally fires his gun during the demonstration
jokes.punchline: He casually admits Mac was right about his hair trigger
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to earlier setup - Mac's warning proves immediately true with dangerous consequences.

jokes.text: Look at all these maniacs. Goddamn bloodthirsty freaks walking in off the street. So easy for anybody to just waltz in and buy a gun.
jokes.setup: Dennis is at a gun store
jokes.punchline: He calls gun buyers maniacs while being there to buy guns himself
jokes.explanation: Dennis's complete lack of self-awareness - judging other gun buyers while being one himself.

jokes.text: I would like to buy a man destroyer from you, please. Hey, maybe you could recommend something that could blow a hole through an elephant, you know. 'Cause I need that for my home protection.
jokes.setup: Dennis and Dee trying to buy guns to prove a point
jokes.punchline: They use increasingly ridiculous descriptions of firepower
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of absurd weapon requests - 'man destroyer' and elephant-piercing power for home defense.

jokes.text: Now, theoretically, Gunther, would I be able to slaughter a roomful of innocent people with that weapon?
jokes.setup: Dennis examining the AR-15
jokes.punchline: He asks about mass murder capability in front of the gun dealer
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy - Dennis casually asking about mass murder capabilities while the dealer responds matter-of-factly.

jokes.text: Can we... can we cut? Will my hands look this small on the screen? I thought the camera would add ten pounds to my hands, and I don't like the way they look.
jokes.setup: Jack Kelly is on live TV as Frank's attorney
jokes.punchline: He's obsessed with how small his hands look on camera
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to Jack Kelly's signature neurosis - completely derailing a serious interview over hand insecurity.

jokes.text: And these liberals like Al Gore, they're trying to take that away from us with this global warming bullshit.
jokes.setup: Jack discussing Second Amendment rights
jokes.punchline: He somehow connects it to Al Gore and global warming
jokes.explanation: Jack's bizarre logic connecting completely unrelated political issues - typical conspiracy theorist behavior.

jokes.text: The government of today has no right telling us how to live our lives, because the government of 200 years ago already did.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining constitutional rights
jokes.punchline: His logic is completely backwards and nonsensical
jokes.explanation: Brilliant nonsensical logic - suggests past government authority somehow prevents current government authority.

jokes.text: They also protected us from tyranny against the seizure of tasteful, artistic photographs of beautiful bodies. Not now, Jack. That same government wants to come into our home, tell us what is and what is not art?
jokes.setup: Jack continuing his constitutional argument
jokes.punchline: He starts talking about his inappropriate photography while Frank tries to stop him
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Jack can't help but bring his creepy photography obsession into a gun rights interview.

jokes.text: It says here you have an extensive history of felonious behavior. Oh, there have been incidents, sure, yeah, a few minor indiscretions, but, you know, I-I'm simply a person of interest in most of those cases, all right?
jokes.setup: Dennis is denied a gun purchase
jokes.punchline: He minimizes his extensive criminal history as 'minor indiscretions'
jokes.explanation: Dennis's narcissistic rationalization of his criminal behavior - typical character deflection.

jokes.text: Being wanted and being 'wanted for questioning' are very, very, very different things, Gunther.
jokes.setup: Dennis defending his criminal record
jokes.punchline: He makes a semantic distinction that doesn't actually matter
jokes.explanation: Dennis trying to lawyer his way out with meaningless distinctions - good character-based wordplay.

jokes.text: Says here you burned her. I burned her, yeah. I burned her. But... She was so annoying. I mean, it... She deserved it, okay?
jokes.setup: Dee's background check reveals she burned someone
jokes.punchline: She casually admits to arson and blames the victim
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation - Dee admits to arson and immediately justifies it with petty complaints about her victim.

jokes.text: You know, you light one bitch on fire, and everybody freaks out!
jokes.setup: Dee is frustrated about being denied the gun
jokes.punchline: She acts like arson is a minor overreaction people have
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dee line - treating arson as a minor social faux pas that people overreact to. Dark and quotable.

jokes.text: Now, pretend that this dummy is a school shooter. I want you to stand back, 'cause I don't want you to get hurt. Before he can draw his weapon... Hie-aught! Hup, hut, hut! Hie!
jokes.setup: Mac demonstrating sword combat against guns
jokes.punchline: He makes ridiculous martial arts sounds while attacking a dummy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac delusion - his absurd martial arts demonstration with sound effects shows his complete disconnect from reality.

jokes.text: Yeah, but did you ever see Indiana Jones, where the guy does the whole sword thing and then he just shoots him dead? Are you asking me if I've seen Indiana Jones? Are you seriously asking me? I've seen that movie... That is my...
jokes.setup: Charlie references Indiana Jones to counter Mac's sword demo
jokes.punchline: Mac is offended that Charlie would question his knowledge of action movies
jokes.explanation: Mac's wounded pride about his action movie expertise - good character moment showing his priorities.

jokes.text: Look at this kid. Ooh, he's wearing all black, he's walking by himself. What do you think he's doing on that phone? Oh, I'll tell you exactly what he's doing. He's playing one of those games where you shoot everyone.
jokes.setup: Mac and Charlie see a kid in black clothes
jokes.punchline: They immediately profile him as a potential threat based on stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Dark satire of profiling and moral panic about video games - shows their ignorant fear-mongering.

jokes.text: Oh! He's killing zombies! I'm taking this. What the hell, man? Hey, kid, you're getting off easy! Now get to school. Go to school. Just know we're watching you.
jokes.setup: They confront the kid about his phone
jokes.punchline: They confiscate his phone for playing a zombie game and threaten continued surveillance
jokes.explanation: Escalation of their vigilante delusion - they're harassing kids based on video games and clothing choices.

jokes.text: I play violent video games. Well, yeah, I also play violent video games. I watch violent movies. Well, yeah, violent movies are the best movies. And I don't want to kill people. No, I mean...
jokes.setup: They realize their profiling logic applies to themselves
jokes.punchline: They immediately contradict their own theory about violent media
jokes.explanation: Good irony - they realize their profiling criteria applies to themselves, forcing them to abandon their logic.

jokes.text: I mean, we're trying to keep the school safe! I know, well, yeah. Well, yeah, we would all feel a lot safer if you were further away.
jokes.setup: Mac insists they're protecting the school
jokes.punchline: The principal says everyone would feel safer if they left
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic reversal - the 'protectors' are seen as the actual threat by the people they're trying to protect.

jokes.text: You know, someone you'd never suspect could stop a shooter. I got an idea.
jokes.setup: Mac realizing they need someone who can be in the school
jokes.punchline: He gets an idea for an unexpected protector
jokes.explanation: Setup for the next scene - moderate effectiveness as a transition joke.

jokes.text: Uh, it's not a problem. We all got a sketchy past. Oh, genuine creep. Wonderful.
jokes.setup: They find a gun seller who doesn't care about backgrounds
jokes.punchline: The seller admits everyone there is sketchy, confirming Dennis's fears
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about gun show culture - the seller's casual admission of criminal clientele.

jokes.text: Oh, this ain't Gunther's. Oh, you see, I'm a private seller. That means I can do whatever I want. Shit.
jokes.setup: They expect the same price as Gunther's store
jokes.punchline: The private seller reveals he can charge whatever he wants
jokes.explanation: Shows how their plan backfires - the unregulated market is actually worse for buyers.

jokes.text: I will burn you alive like the last bitch who crossed me! Okay, all right, all right, Dee. That's not getting us anywhere. Okay, she's hot.
jokes.setup: Dee gets angry at the price gouging
jokes.punchline: She threatens arson again, and Dennis says 'she's hot' referring to both anger and temperature
jokes.explanation: Good callback to both Dee's arson tendencies and the ongoing 'hot' wordplay from the episode.

jokes.text: How about I just take it, huh? 'Cause I'm a maniac with a gun, and there's nothing you can do about it.
jokes.setup: Dennis gets frustrated with the high prices
jokes.punchline: He threatens armed robbery to prove guns are dangerous
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - Dennis becomes the exact threat he's trying to argue against, proving everyone's point.

jokes.text: But I see now that I was the maniac with the gun. And you all made it safer... in here. Yeah. Good for you.
jokes.setup: Dennis is surrounded by armed gun show attendees
jokes.punchline: He realizes everyone being armed stopped his threat
jokes.explanation: Brilliant ironic reversal - Dennis accidentally proves the 'good guy with a gun' argument he opposes.

jokes.text: Yo, can I get some beer? No beer. No. We said it, like, a million times. Guys, guys, how many times do we have to be through this? No beer, okay?
jokes.setup: Mac and Charlie are training kids for school safety
jokes.punchline: The kids keep asking for beer despite repeated denials
jokes.explanation: Running gag establishing the kids' priorities and Mac/Charlie's poor authority.

jokes.text: But you guys had, like, five. Don't count beers, Carlos. Not cool. Yeah. And by the way, Carlos, I've had six. And I've had seven.
jokes.setup: Carlos points out their hypocrisy about drinking
jokes.punchline: Mac and Charlie competitively reveal they've had even more beer
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing their complete lack of authority and self-control while training children.

jokes.text: Look at the person sitting next to you. That person wants to take you out.
jokes.setup: Mac is instructing the armed children
jokes.punchline: He tells them to view each other as threats
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation - Mac creates paranoia among armed children, setting up the inevitable chaos.

jokes.text: Those-those kids are maniacs! Oh, my God, I think they're all in gangs or something! There's a lot of score settling going on in there! Oh, yeah, dude, we created a Hunger Games-type scenario in there.
jokes.setup: The children's training has devolved into chaos
jokes.punchline: Mac and Charlie realize they created a battle royale situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to their terrible plan - they accidentally recreated dystopian fiction with real children.

jokes.text: Oh! Oh, shit, Charlie, I got to admit something, dude. I'm bleeding pretty bad. One of the kids got you? I nicked myself back in the principal's office just trying to upholster my weapon.
jokes.setup: Mac appears to be injured from the children's chaos
jokes.punchline: He reveals he accidentally hurt himself earlier trying to holster his gun
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Mac's incompetence with weapons - he's been bleeding the whole time from his own mistake.

jokes.text: You got my people-killer? Right here. Oh, ho, ho, ho. That's the stuff, baby.
jokes.setup: Dennis is buying a gun from a street dealer
jokes.punchline: He excitedly refers to it as a 'people-killer'
jokes.explanation: Dennis's enthusiasm for the weapon despite his supposed anti-gun stance - shows his hypocrisy.

jokes.text: Well, sir, I gave you the $1,500. Don't saunter away. I'm try... That's not fair! No, it's not fair! You stop right now!
jokes.setup: Dennis realizes he's been scammed by the street dealer
jokes.punchline: He uselessly yells at the armed criminal walking away
jokes.explanation: Dennis's privileged entitlement meets criminal reality - he expects fairness from a street gun dealer.

jokes.text: The man has an assault rifle! What am I supposed to do?! Shit! I can't buy one! The only thing that stops a bad guy with a gun is a good guy with a gun, which we can't get!
jokes.setup: Dee tells Dennis to stop the scammer
jokes.punchline: Dennis repeats NRA talking points while being victimized
jokes.explanation: Brilliant ironic moment - Dennis parrots gun advocate slogans while being robbed by someone with a gun.

jokes.text: Goddamn son of a bitch Frank, you were right! Frank was right! Shit!
jokes.setup: Dennis has been victimized trying to prove guns are too easy to buy
jokes.punchline: He concludes Frank was right about needing guns
jokes.explanation: Good character reversal - Dennis's experience completely flips his position, showing how easily swayed he is.

jokes.text: I think we crossed right past each other like ships in the night, man. We flip-flopped. Yeah, 'cause we're on the opposite side now.
jokes.setup: The gang meets back up after their experiences
jokes.punchline: They realize they've completely switched positions on guns
jokes.explanation: Good structural callback - the episode comes full circle with everyone having switched sides.

jokes.text: Guns? No. Water filters. Water filters? All right, guys, load 'em in. Very nice.
jokes.setup: Frank appears to have a truck full of guns
jokes.punchline: He reveals they're water filters for his next scam
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - sets up Frank's next scam reveal while subverting expectations about the truck contents.

jokes.text: I bought a stake in Gunther's Guns. I got everybody angry and scared. They bought the guns, I made a fortune.
jokes.setup: Frank explains his sudden anti-gun stance
jokes.punchline: He reveals the whole thing was a profit scheme
jokes.explanation: Excellent reveal of Frank's manipulation - recontextualizes the entire episode as his profit scheme.

jokes.text: This is America: you're either a duper or a dupee. I'm a duper. You guys are the dupees.
jokes.setup: Frank explaining his philosophy
jokes.punchline: He categorizes everyone as either a con artist or a victim
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank philosophy - cynical but accurate observation about American capitalism and his relationship to the gang.

jokes.text: Do you know that 90% of your water is 100% toxins? Who knows what the other 10% is? It's probably far worse, I would imagine. It's really worse.
jokes.setup: Frank promoting water filters on TV
jokes.punchline: His math doesn't add up and he suggests the unknown 10% is worse than toxins
jokes.explanation: Frank's nonsensical fear-mongering - mathematically impossible percentages and illogical escalation.

jokes.text: I think I'm gonna get one of those water filters. I do kind of want one, right? For safety, for safety. I mean, he does make some good points. Yeah. The water's toxic. I mean, it is. Yeah.
jokes.setup: Frank's water filter pitch on TV
jokes.punchline: The gang immediately falls for his new scam despite knowing it's a scam
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending - the gang immediately proves Frank's point by falling for his next scam despite understanding his methods.
